$comment: Qualifying + career-milestone pack (prefix qm.): era-voiced pole coverage, split positive/negative qualifying upsets, streak escalation tiers, drought epics, per-counter career milestones, and second-take features for the career firsts so the rendezvous pick varies between careers. Follows 010-core.json format exactly; read-side only.

1960s: The timekeepers' ledger admits no argument.
One flying lap, hand-timed and final.
1980s: One lap, everything turned up, no prisoners.
Saturday is for the brave, and the grid sheet proves it.
2010s: The timing screens told the story before the pit wall could.
smgp: ONE LAP TO RULE THE BOARD.
default: The grid sheet is the weekend's first verdict.
Saturday pace is a promise; Sunday decides whether it is kept.
Qualifying tells the truth one lap at a time.

1960s: The scribes have taken to counting on their fingers.
Runs like this are the stuff of annuals and after-dinner speeches.
2010s: The broadcast graphics team already has the streak counter built for next round.
The number is the story now, and everyone in the paddock knows it.
smgp: THE LADDER FEARS A HOT HAND.
default: Runs like this bend a whole season around them.
The paddock has started keeping count out loud.
Streaks change how rivals race you - carefully.

1960s: The old hands nodded; every one of them remembers their own first.
A fresh ledger has its first entry inked.
2010s: The clip will do numbers by midnight.
Somewhere a highlights editor just saved the file twice.
smgp: A NEW LINE ON THE BIG BOARD.
THE CROWD LEARNS A NEW NAME.
default: Careers are built one first at a time.
Nobody forgets where they were for the first one.
The record book opens a fresh page and writes a name at the top.

1960s: The mechanics permitted themselves a smile, which is saying something.
1990s: The press room checked the databases twice before believing it.
The statisticians closed one long-running file tonight.
smgp: THE BOARD REMEMBERS HOW TO SMILE.
default: Relief has a sound, and the garage made it.
The zero is gone; the season breathes again.

templates.headline: Fastest time of the day: {subject} heads the grid[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: Qualifying report, round {round} of {year}
templates.summary: {subject} set the fastest qualifying time for round {round}.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The timekeepers have completed their arithmetic and the answer is {subject}: fastest of all in practice for round {round}[[?venue: at {venue}]], and first away when the flag drops.
sections.context: {pool:qmGridColor} In an age when the watches are held by hand and the margins are read in tenths, heading the sheet is a plain statement of speed, and the paddock treats it as such.
sections.close: The grid forms up behind {player's} machine, and the rest must make their arrangements accordingly.

templates.headline: {subject} steals pole[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: Qualifying, round {round}: the front row has an owner
templates.summary: {subject} took pole position for round {round}.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} In a session that rewarded the brave and punished everyone else, {subject} hung it all out when the track was at its quickest and came away with pole[[?venue: at {venue}]].
sections.context: {pool:qmGridColor} These are years when a pole lap means something animal - wings, slicks and horsepower barely on speaking terms, and a driver willing to arbitrate.

templates.headline: POLE! {subject} blitzes qualifying[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: Round {round}: the front row starts here
templates.summary: {subject} grabbed pole for round {round}.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Strap in. {subject} threw one monster lap at the clock[[?venue: at {venue}]] and nobody answered it. Pole position, round {round}, end of argument.
sections.stats: {pool:qmGridColor} One lap on maximum attack settles more arguments than a season of press conferences.
sections.close: Sunday's question just got simpler: who is brave enough to take it off {player}?

templates.headline: {subject} takes pole[[?venue: at {venue}]] - and it was not close
templates.deck: Qualifying report, round {round}
templates.summary: {subject} topped qualifying for round {round}.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Quickest when it mattered: {subject} went to the top of the timing screens on the final runs and stayed there[[?venue: , locking down pole at {venue}]].
sections.stats: {pool:qmGridColor} [[?champPosition: The championship account currently reads {ord:champPosition}, and Sundays started from the front have a way of improving it.]]
sections.next: Lights out will tell us whether the race pace matches the headline lap.

templates.headline: POLE POSITION! {subject} OWNS THE CLOCK[[?venue: AT {venue}]]
templates.deck: ROUND {round}: THE FRONT SPOT IS TAKEN
templates.summary: {subject} takes pole for round {round}.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} THE TIMING BOARD FLASHES ONE NAME: {subject}. FASTEST LAP OF QUALIFYING. FIRST LIGHT ON THE GRID FOR ROUND {round}.
sections.impact: {pool:qmGridColor} EVERY RIVAL SAW THE LAP. NONE OF THEM MATCHED IT.

templates.headline: {subject} takes pole in the rain[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: A soaked qualifying session finds its master
templates.summary: {subject} took pole in wet qualifying for round {round}.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} On a day when most of the field qualified with their elbows, {subject} qualified with fingertips: pole position on a streaming track[[?venue: at {venue}]], where every quick lap was a small act of defiance.
sections.context: Wet qualifying strips the sport to its oldest question - who trusts the car least and themselves most. The answer sits first on the grid for round {round}.

templates.headline: One lap changes everything: a first pole for {subject}
templates.deck: The breakthrough Saturday of {player's} career
templates.summary: {subject} claimed a maiden pole position in round {round}.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} There was a moment today when the whole timing sheet reorganised itself under one name, and the name was {subject}. First career pole[[?venue: , at {venue},]] and the grid suddenly reads differently.
sections.stats: {pool:qmGridColor} Poles are the purest data point in racing: no strategy, no luck, no inheritance. From today, {player's} single-lap ceiling is a matter of public record.
sections.next: The harder trick is doing it again. The paddock suspects this was not a one-off.

templates.headline: Where did that come from? {subject} storms to {ord:quali} on the grid
templates.deck: Qualifying upset[[?venue: at {venue}]]: the form book takes a beating
templates.summary: {subject} qualified a stunning {ord:quali}[[?expected: against an expected {ord:expected}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Someone forgot to tell {subject} where the car is supposed to qualify. {ord:quali} on the grid for round {round}[[?expected: , a full leap on the {ord:expected} the paddock had pencilled in]], and the timing sheet has some explaining to do.
sections.context: {pool:qmGridColor} Laps like this are either a setup gamble that landed or a driver operating above the machinery. Both readings make Sunday considerably more interesting.
sections.next: Holding the position over race distance is another matter entirely - which is exactly why the grandstands will be watching that part of the grid.

templates.headline: Sensation on Saturday: {subject} lines up {ord:quali}
templates.deck: One of the qualifying shocks of the season[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} produced a sensational {ord:quali} in qualifying[[?expected: from an expected {ord:expected}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Every season produces one Saturday that nobody's model predicted. This was it: {subject}, {ord:quali} on the grid[[?expected: where the form said {ord:expected}]], and a pit lane full of engineers rechecking their data for an error that is not there.
sections.stats: Upsets of this size are not tow-and-track-position flukes; the lap had to be built in every sector, and it was. The onboard will be studied up and down the paddock tonight.

templates.headline: Alarm bells: {subject} buried down in {ord:quali}
templates.deck: A qualifying session goes badly wrong[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} qualified only {ord:quali}[[?expected: against an expected {ord:expected}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The grid sheet made grim reading in {team's} garage: {subject} starts round {round} from {ord:quali}[[?expected: , nowhere near the {ord:expected} the pace suggested was available]]. Somewhere between the garage and the flying lap, the speed went missing.
sections.context: The debrief has one job tonight: decide whether this was the car, the lap or the traffic - because the answer changes what Sunday can still rescue.
sections.next: Race day now becomes recovery work: overtakes, strategy and a measure of luck, in roughly that order.

templates.headline: Make it {streak}: {subject} will not stop winning
templates.deck: The streak rolls on[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} extended the winning run to {streak} races.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} There is a particular quiet that settles over a paddock when one crew keeps winning. It was audible again today: {subject}, first again[[?venue: at {venue}]], {streak} in succession, and no obvious end in sight.
sections.rivalry: [[?rival: Somewhere down the pit lane, {rival} is running out of polite answers to the same question.]] {pool:qmStreakBuzz}

templates.headline: This is domination: {streak} straight wins for {subject}
templates.deck: A season bends around one garage
templates.summary: {subject} has won {streak} consecutive races - domination territory.

sections.lead: Somewhere around the third win, a streak stops being form and starts being an era. At {streak} consecutive victories, {subject} is not competing in this championship so much as administering it.
sections.stats: Runs of five and more belong to a short and serious list. Whatever the ingredients - car, driver, team, nerve - all of them are present at once, and none of the rivals can find the missing one in their own garage.

templates.headline: {subject} banks {a:ord:streak} straight podium
templates.deck: Consistency becomes a habit[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} has finished on the podium {streak} races running.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The rostrum is starting to feel like assigned seating: {subject} collected {a:ord:streak} consecutive podium[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?position: with {ord:position}]], and the points keep arriving in fat envelopes.
sections.championship: [[?champPosition: {ord:champPosition} in the table, and climbing the honest way.]] {pool:champChatter}

templates.headline: The iron run: {streak} podiums in a row for {subject}
templates.deck: One of the great consistency streaks
templates.summary: {subject} extended a remarkable podium run to {streak} races.

sections.lead: Anyone can have a weekend. {streak} consecutive podiums is a season-within-a-season, and it is the kind of record engineers respect more than wins: it means the car was right, the driver was right, and nothing broke - over and over again.
sections.stats: Streaks of this length survive bad Saturdays, wrong tyre calls and first-lap chaos. That is what makes them rare, and what makes rivals quietly furious.

templates.headline: {streak} rounds, {streak} scores: {subject} is the safest bet in the paddock
templates.deck: The scoring run rolls on
templates.summary: {subject} has scored in {streak} consecutive rounds.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Championships are lost on zeroes, and {subject} has forgotten how to write one: {streak} consecutive rounds in the points after today[[?venue: at {venue}]], every weekend converted, nothing thrown away.
sections.championship: {pool:champChatter} [[?champPosition: The standings say {ord:champPosition}; the trend line says better.]]

templates.headline: Winner again: {subject} ends the wait
templates.deck: A first win in {drought} races[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} won for the first time in {drought} races.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The weight is off: {subject} stood on the top step again today[[?venue: at {venue}]], a first victory in {drought} races, and the celebration had the particular volume of something long postponed.
sections.context: Winless runs distort everything around a driver - the questions get slower and crueller, the near-misses heavier. All of it evaporated somewhere on the final lap.

templates.headline: After {drought} races, the longest wait of {player's} career is over
templates.deck: An epic drought ends[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} ended a winless run of {drought} races.

sections.lead: Some waits get so long they become part of a driver's biography. {drought} races - seasons of Sundays - separated {player's} last win from this one, and when the flag finally fell[[?venue: at {venue}]] the garage did not cheer so much as let go.
sections.context: Droughts of this length end careers more often than they end in victory. That this one finished the right way says everything about the stubbornness involved - driver, team, all of it.
sections.impact: [[?team: Inside {team} they will tell you nobody stopped believing. That is never quite true, which makes the ones who kept working all the more remarkable.]]

templates.headline: The famine ends: {subject} scores after {drought} empty races
templates.deck: A points drought of rare length is finally over
templates.summary: {subject} scored for the first time in {drought} races.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} For {drought} consecutive races the results column gave {subject} nothing at all. Today it finally paid out[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?position: - {ord:position}, and rarely has a modest finishing position been welcomed like a trophy]].
sections.context: Runs like that hollow a season out from the inside. Breaking one is less a result than a rescue.

templates.headline: Win number {milestone}: {subject} joins the big numbers
templates.deck: A landmark victory total
templates.summary: {subject} reached {milestone} career wins.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Victories are the currency of this sport, and {subject} just banked a round number: career win number {milestone}[[?venue: , delivered at {venue}]]. The company at that mark is select, and it knows it.
sections.stats: Count them back and every one tells a different story - the easy ones, the stolen ones, the ones that arrived in the rain. Totals like {milestone} are careers in shorthand.

templates.headline: {milestone} starts: the long service of {subject}
templates.deck: A durability milestone for the record books
templates.summary: {subject} made {a:ord:milestone} career start.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Nobody accumulates {milestone} Grand Prix starts by accident. It takes speed enough to be wanted, toughness enough to keep the seat, and a plain love of the thing - {subject} lined up for the {ord:milestone} time today[[?venue: at {venue}]].
sections.context: Start counts are the sport's quietest statistic and its most honest: every single one had to be earned twice, once with a contract and once with a crash helmet.

templates.headline: {milestone} podiums for {subject}
templates.deck: The silverware shelf reaches a round number
templates.summary: {subject} reached {milestone} career podiums.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The trophy cabinet needed a new shelf anyway: {subject} collected a {ord:milestone} career podium today[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?position: with {ord:position}]].
sections.stats: Podium totals measure something wins alone miss - the ability to be there, weekend after weekend, whatever the machinery allows. {milestone} of them is a body of work.

templates.headline: New ceiling: {ord:position} is the best of {player's} career
templates.deck: A personal record falls in round {round}
templates.summary: {subject} set a career-best finish of {ord:position}.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Careers move in steps, and {subject} just climbed one: {ord:position}[[?venue: at {venue}]] is the highest {player} has ever finished, and the old mark is now just a line in the history.
sections.next: Personal bests have a way of resetting ambitions. The target now is simple: make today's ceiling tomorrow's floor.

templates.headline: Chapter one: {player's} championship story begins
templates.deck: Debut[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?team: for {team}]], round {round} of {year}
templates.summary: {subject} started a first championship race.

sections.lead: Before the statistics, before the reputation, before any of it, there is a single unremarkable line on an entry list. {player's} line was printed this week, and today it turned into a racing career[[?venue: on the grid at {venue}]].
sections.context: [[?team: {team} took the chance; the driver repays it one clean weekend at a time.]] Debuts are not judged on results but on what the garage says afterwards - and garages notice everything.

templates.headline: {subject} is on the board
templates.deck: First career points arrive in round {round}
templates.summary: First championship points for {subject}[[?position: with {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} There is a club with no ceremony and a strict entrance requirement, and {subject} joined it today: championship scorers[[?position: , via {a:ord:position} place]][[?venue: at {venue}]].
sections.impact: Zeroes stop meaning never and start meaning not this week - that is what a first score changes. [[?team: For {team}, it is also the first hard evidence that the investment works.]]

templates.headline: Knocking on the door: a first top-five for {subject}
templates.deck: The front of the field gets a new neighbour
templates.summary: {subject} recorded a first career top-five finish[[?position: with {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The gap between the midfield and the front is the widest in racing, and {subject} just crossed it: a first top-five finish[[?position: , {ord:position},]][[?venue: at {venue}]] - close enough to read the podium's paintwork.
sections.next: The order of a career's firsts rarely changes: top five, then the rostrum, then the champagne gets expensive. The next box is now in plain view.

templates.headline: The view from up there: {player's} first podium
templates.deck: A maiden rostrum in round {round}, {year}
templates.summary: {subject} claimed a first career podium[[?position: with {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: Ask any driver what they remember about their first podium and they never say the trophy - they talk about the view: the crowd where the track used to be, the two others pretending they always knew. From today, {subject} has that memory[[?venue: , earned at {venue}]].
sections.context: [[?team: In {team's} garage the photographs are already being chosen.]] {pool:qmFirstsNote}
sections.next: First podiums redraw the target: the middle step is a rung, not a summit.

templates.headline: FIRST TROPHY! {subject} REACHES THE ROSTRUM
templates.deck: A MAIDEN PODIUM LIGHTS UP ROUND {round}
templates.summary: {subject} landed a first career podium[[?position: with {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} A NEW FACE ON THE PODIUM GRAPHIC! {subject} CLIMBED THE STEPS FOR THE FIRST TIME[[?position: - {ord:position} PLACE -]] AND THE CROWD MADE THE SPEAKERS REDUNDANT.
sections.impact: FIRST TROPHIES CHANGE EVERYTHING. THE LADDER HAS A NEW CLIMBER, AND EVERY RIVAL JUST FELT IT.

templates.headline: Anatomy of a first win: how {subject} finally broke through
templates.deck: Feature: the making of a maiden victory
templates.summary: The story behind {player's} first career win.

sections.lead: First wins look sudden and never are. Today's[[?venue: victory at {venue}]] was assembled from years of parts: the junior seasons, the near-misses, the Sundays that taught what pressure does at the front.
sections.stats: The record will show one line - round {round}, {year}, first place. The record is efficient and wrong; this was never one afternoon's work.
sections.impact: [[?team: {team} takes the constructor's share of the credit, and has earned it - maiden wins are team results wearing one driver's name.]]
sections.next: Every new winner is asked the same thing within the hour: can you do it again? The good ones do not answer with words.

templates.headline: FLASH: maiden victory for {subject}[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: First career win confirmed in round {round}
templates.summary: {subject} has won for the first time - confirmed.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Confirmed from race control: {subject} wins round {round}[[?venue: at {venue}]] - the first victory of {player's} championship career. [[?team: A first for the driver, another for {team}.]] Full timing to follow.
sections.context: The result stands as posted. The celebrations, by all accounts, will not be as brief as this bulletin.

templates.headline: Welcome to the other column: {subject} retires for the first time
templates.deck: Every career meets this day eventually
templates.summary: {subject} recorded a first career retirement.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Until today, {player's} record had a strange purity to it: started everything, finished everything. That ended in round {round}[[?venue: at {venue}]], and the education is the better for it.
sections.context: Old mechanics say a driver does not really join the sport until it has broken their heart once. Consider the membership complete.
